verses.text: The angel shows a golden lampstand fed by a bowl, with seven lamps and two olive trees beside. A picture of supply. God's light is kept burning by His own provision.
The angel shows a golden lampstand fed by a bowl, with seven lamps and two olive trees beside. A picture of supply. God's light is kept burning by His own provision.
The angel shows a golden lampstand fed by a bowl, with seven lamps and two olive trees beside. A picture of supply. God's light is kept burning by His own provision.
'What are these, my lord?' the prophet asks; 'Knowest thou not?' 'No, my lord.' Humble ignorance. It is no shame to confess we do not yet understand God's ways.
'What are these, my lord?' the prophet asks; 'Knowest thou not?' 'No, my lord.' Humble ignorance. It is no shame to confess we do not yet understand God's ways.
'Not by might, nor by power, but by my spirit, saith the LORD'; the great mountain shall become a plain. The secret of the work. God's work is done by His Spirit, not by human strength.
'Not by might, nor by power, but by my spirit, saith the LORD'; the great mountain shall become a plain. The secret of the work. God's work is done by His Spirit, not by human strength.
Zerubbabel's hands laid the foundation and shall finish it; 'who hath despised the day of small things?' Small beginnings. God delights to complete what looks unpromising at the start.
Zerubbabel's hands laid the foundation and shall finish it; 'who hath despised the day of small things?' Small beginnings. God delights to complete what looks unpromising at the start.
Zerubbabel's hands laid the foundation and shall finish it; 'who hath despised the day of small things?' Small beginnings. God delights to complete what looks unpromising at the start.
The two olive trees are 'the two anointed ones, that stand by the Lord of the whole earth.' Anointed servants. God supplies His work through those He fills with His Spirit.
